triggers: A user asks to review code changes, a PR, or a diff
A user requests a security audit or correctness check on specific files

activation_triggers: A user asks to review code changes, a PR, or a diff
A user requests a security audit or correctness check on specific files
execution_steps: Identify the review target (files, diff, PR).
Read the changed or specified code.
Analyze for the requested focus: correctness, security, performance, maintainability, or API risk.
Report only findings that meet the severity bar.
Cite specific file paths and line ranges as evidence.
forbidden_actions: Do not print full secret values.
Do not push, deploy, or call external services.
output_requirements: Each finding: severity, file, line range, description, evidence
Optional: suggested fixes (reported separately, not applied)

stages.raw:
{
  "activation_triggers": [
    "A user asks to review code changes, a PR, or a diff",
    "A user requests a security audit or correctness check on specific files"
  ],
  "execution_steps": [
    "Identify the review target (files, diff, PR).",
    "Read the changed or specified code.",
    "Analyze for the requested focus: correctness, security, performance, maintainability, or API risk.",
    "Report only findings that meet the severity bar.",
    "Cite specific file paths and line ranges as evidence."
  ],
  "output_requirements": [
    "A structured review report",
    "Each finding: severity, file, line range, description, evidence",
    "Optional: suggested fixes (reported separately, not applied)"
  ],
  "forbidden_actions": [
    "Do not modify any files.",
    "Do not speculate.",
    "Do not print full secret values.",
    "Do not push, deploy, or call external services."
  ],
  "verification_statements": [],
  "safety_constraints": [
    "Review is read-only.",
    "Only report evidence-backed findings.",
    "Redact secret-like values in evidence."
  ]
}

parsed.description: The specific files, diff, or pull request to be reviewed. This could be file paths, a diff string, or a PR identifier.

evidence.quote: Identify the review target (files, diff, PR).
evidence.rationale: The first execution step explicitly requires identifying the review target, meaning the agent must receive this information before starting.

parsed.name: review_target

parsed.description: The specific aspect(s) to analyze: correctness, security, performance, maintainability, or API risk. The user may specify one or more of these.

evidence.quote: Analyze for the requested focus: correctness, security, performance, maintainability, or API risk.
evidence.rationale: The execution step requires analyzing for a 'requested focus', implying the user must specify which focus areas are desired.

parsed.name: review_focus

parsed.description: The minimum severity level for findings to be reported (e.g., 'critical only', 'high and above', 'all').

evidence.quote: Report only findings that meet the severity bar.
evidence.rationale: The agent needs to know what the 'severity bar' is in order to filter findings appropriately.

parsed.name: severity_bar

parsed.description: The actual source code content of the files, diff, or PR to be reviewed. This is needed to perform the analysis.

evidence.quote: Read the changed or specified code.
evidence.rationale: The execution step requires reading the code, so the agent must have access to the code content.

parsed.name: code_content
